entries.requested: Bring up D_Cortex training locally on Windows + RTX 5080 (sm_120), adapt colab/step2_training_v11.py to single-GPU local execution, run a real training run with 4 verification gates.
entries.executed: Explored repo (dcortex package, colab v11 script, config/model). Verified repo model is compatible with v11 training loop. Verified GPU at driver level: RTX 5080, driver 610.47, compute_cap 12.0, 16303 MiB. Confirmed base python 3.11.9 has torch 2.4.1+cpu only (no CUDA). Inspected local dataset E:\DATA\training_data_clean ({"text":...} jsonl).
entries.notes: Reflect-back delivered. Highest training ref = v11. Model exposes encode/decode/reset_memory/begin_episode/clear_overlays + shared_* + aux_answer_head.

entries.requested: Resolve dataset contradiction (header E:\DATA path vs body 'keep TinyStories').
entries.executed: Asked architectural question. User chose: use local E:\DATA\training_data_clean (BYON jsonl) for the 25% LM slice, via the same tokenize-to-.bin pipeline, fully offline. Structural episodes remain synthetic either way.
entries.notes: Decision recorded in project_concept.json dataset.lm_corpus.

entries.requested: Set up environment and project memory before coding.
entries.executed: Kicked off background cu128 venv build (.venv, py 3.11, torch>=2.7 cu128 + numpy/tiktoken/datasets/matplotlib). Created .claude/ with project_concept.json, project_structure.json, project_log.json.
entries.notes: Install running in background (torch cu128 wheel is large). Next: write verify_env_local.py and train_local.py, then run gates.

entries.requested: Run Gate 1 ENV and adapt the v11 training script for local single-GPU Windows.
entries.executed: Installed torch 2.11.0+cu128 (cuda 12.8). Ran scripts/verify_env_local.py: PASS. Wrote colab/train_local.py (drops Drive mount and inline write_source, imports real dcortex package, local PROJECT_ROOT, local E:\DATA LM tokenizer, bf16/no-GradScaler, gradient checkpointing on decoder StandardTransformerBlock via training-side wrap, atomic _step_ checkpoints with numeric resume + RNG + loss_history, OOM guard, aggregate train_loss, peak VRAM tracking). Wrote scripts/emit_verdict.py.
entries.notes: ENV PASS source: PowerShell run of verify_env_local.py. Evidence: cuda available; RTX 5080; capability 12.0; bf16 4096x4096 matmul OK; torch=2.11.0+cu128. Total VRAM reported 15.92 GB.

entries.requested: Smoke-test the adapted script before the real run.
entries.executed: 12-step smoke run exposed a real Windows bug: os.rename raises FileExistsError when the destination checkpoint exists (POSIX replaces, Windows does not). Fixed save_ckpt to use os.replace (atomic on both OSes) and guarded the redundant final save. Re-ran two-phase smoke (fresh->6, resume->12): resume reproduced the continuous trajectory BIT-IDENTICALLY (step 6 loss 6.2928 in both), loss_history restored (n_logged 12), resumed=true.
entries.notes: Source: PowerShell smoke runs, run_meta_smoke_p1/p2.json. RNG restore in checkpoint makes resume continuation bit-identical to a continuous run.

entries.requested: Run the real 300-step training (Gate 2 FIT, Gate 3 LEARNS).
entries.executed: Ran colab/train_local.py --total-steps 300 --max-steps 300 fresh in runs/dcortex on the local E:\DATA BYON corpus (5M train / 0.5M val tokens). Completed 300 steps in 7.81 min, 0 OOM, checkpoints at 100/200/300.
entries.notes: VERIFIED numbers (source: runs/dcortex/results/run_meta_main.json + background task bocqguly8). FIT: peak alloc 3.234 GB, peak@step50 3.234 GB, peak reserved 3.711 GB, OOM 0 -> well under 15 GB target. LEARNS: loss_first 11.7897, loss_last 3.0807, loss_min 2.1056, loss_max 11.7897; emit 6.043 -> 1.317. dtype bfloat16, gradient_checkpointing true. Final verdict JSON pending RESUME gate + emit_verdict run.

entries.requested: Run Gate 4 RESUME.
entries.executed: First two RESUME attempts crashed in phase 1 (~step 25, exit 1/-1, NO Python traceback). Diagnosed via nvidia-smi: GPU contention from an orphaned python from an earlier foreground attempt (piping native exe into Select-Object terminated the parent but left the child training process alive on the GPU). Stopped background tasks, killed stray pythons (GPU back to 1302 MiB / 1%), re-launched RESUME with exclusive GPU access (phase1 fresh->100 with ckpts at 50/100, phase2 resume->150).
entries.notes: Not a training-math bug: the main 300-step run used the same seed 42 and survived those exact steps. Root cause = concurrent GPU processes. RESUME re-run in progress (task b62ig9tlc); verdict pending.

entries.requested: Complete Gate 4 RESUME with exclusive GPU and emit the final 4-gate verdict JSON + loss curve.
entries.executed: RESUME re-run on a clean GPU succeeded: phase1 fresh->100 (ckpts 50,100, P1_EXIT=0), phase2 resumed from ckpt_dcortex_step_000100.pt at step 100 and ran ->150 (P2_EXIT=0). Ran scripts/emit_verdict.py: ALL 4 GATES PASS. Wrote runs/dcortex/results/verdict.json and loss_curve.png.
entries.notes: VERIFIED verdict (source: runs/dcortex/results/verdict.json, emit_verdict.py task b6qs4391d). ENV PASS (cap 12.0, bf16 matmul). FIT PASS (peak 3.234 GB < 15 GB). LEARNS PASS (drop 68.9%; smoothed 10.6258->3.3045; raw 11.7897->3.0807; min 2.1056, median 3.9417, max 11.7897). RESUME PASS (resumed from step 100, first post-resume loss 4.1651, on-trajectory, not reset). Deliverable complete.

Corpus SHA 0d58ed20b5fb57ad.

entries.notes: ALL 7 GATES PASS on corrected honest holdout. Held-out-predicate exact median 97.1% [95.1%/98.8%] std 0.011 (20 seeds); relative 99.6%/0.4% (was 63%/30.9%); concessive 93.1%; held-out relation currency_code 96.8%; wrong median 2.8% vs RB3 31.4%; CPU repro delta 0.1%. Data-starved hypothesis CONFIRMED. HONEST SCOPE: simple explicitly-cued bindings, cue-predicate axis (not arbitrary syntax), CPU-vs-CUDA same machine (not distinct hardware). MEASURED single-machine, NOT PROVEN. Corpus SHA 408d64a9b87baabb.

entries.requested: v-next+3 FINAL: scale corpus with GENUINE structural diversity, separate cue-predicate from structural generalization, no-memory causal control, compositional holdout, pilot-calibrated thresholds, failure taxonomy, seed-variance localization, 2 environments, 11 frozen gates. Architecture unchanged, seals byte-identical.
entries.executed: Built scripts/build_role_struct_corpus.py (3 pinned relations; globally-disjoint entity pools; cue_predicate vs structural[long_gap,embedded] holdout; compositional appositive; separate entity-disjoint calibration). scripts/validate_role_struct_corpus.py: per-cell 0% baseline, entity disjointness, structural distinctness all PASS. Fixed seal CRLF/LF working-tree mismatch (git blobs untouched) and re-based branch onto sealed role-binding lineage. scripts/certify_role_struct.py: pilot(4)+ENV1 cuda(20)+ENV2 cpu(8), no-memory control, failure taxonomy, variance diagnosis, 11 gates.
entries.notes: ALL 11 GATES PASS. Held-out exact median 99.3% [97.0%/99.9%] std 0.008 (20 seeds). Structural: long_gap 100.0%, embedded 98.8% (verified distinct gap 47 vs 10, depth 8 vs 0). cue_predicate 98.2%; compositional 100.0%; held-out relation currency_code 99.8%. NO-MEMORY control 51.6% -> causal gap +47.7pp. CPU repro delta 0.1%. Pilot froze G_RELATIVE floor 94%, G_COMPOSITIONAL 70%. Corpus SHA 53a032aedc9bdcf2. HONEST: position-blind binder + robust substrate; structural axis = robustness not syntax; CPU-vs-CUDA same machine; MEASURED single-org, NOT PROVEN.

entries.requested: Complete professional D_Cortex integration: mechanical control layer (constrained decoding + hard unbypassable verifier veto), deterministic baseline, neural binder path on target hidden states, attribution tracing, binder-vs-baseline benchmark, gates G1-G11, first pack D_Cortex_PatentAnalyst.

Built D_Cortex_PatentAnalyst pack (10 committed/2 provisional/1 disputed/6 forbidden, repo-grounded provenance). certify_professional.py runs G1-G11. Caught and fixed a crippled-baseline artifact in G11 (entity-only lookup 33% inflated binder to +64.71pp; proper (entity,attribute) lookup is 100% and the binder does not beat it).
entries.notes: VERDICT D_CORTEX_PROFESSIONAL_CAMPAIGN_PASS. ALL 11 GATES PASS. Mechanical grounding demonstrated: all 13 committed answers overridden (raw undertrained substrate emits gibberish; constraint forces committed value). Hallucination rate 0% on unanswerable+ood; 0 leaks across 29 adversarial. G11 HONEST: binder 98.0% does NOT beat proper (entity,attribute) lookup 100% (gap -1.9pp); naive entity-only lookup 33% was a crippled-baseline artifact, caught and corrected. MEASURED single-env; target = undertrained D_Cortex substrate; binder model-specific; NOT production/multi-model claim.

entries.requested: Port forced-grounding to a REAL open model + REAL-scale pack; measure RAW vs CONTROLLED hallucination; full organism (control layer, deterministic baseline, neural binder on real hidden states, attribution, binder-vs-baseline two regimes, gates).
entries.executed: Real base model gpt2-large (HF, fp32, hidden states). Real-scale pack D_Cortex_IPCAnalyst from official WIPO IPC class titles (thxare/IPCcodes@0ff722fb pinned, cleaned, 192 codes -> 380 committed facts). dcortex_professional/hf_runtime.py (constrained decoding + hidden states), ipc_binder.py (two-regime binder), certify_professional_real.py (RAW vs CONTROLLED + 10 gates). Fixed two harness/verifier bugs (over-aggressive contamination false-veto; case bug leaking a committed code into unanswerable). Caught and corrected a strawman binder comparison (exact-lookup 0% -> added fair fuzzy lookup).
entries.notes: VERDICT D_CORTEX_REAL_PROFESSIONAL_PASS. ALL 10 GATES PASS. HEADLINE: RAW 98.8% -> CONTROLLED 1.2% hallucination (delta +97.7%) on gpt2-large + WIPO IPC pack. RAW answerable grounded 0%; controlled recall 95%. BINDER honest negative: near-chance on gpt2-large IPC hidden states (structured 55% vs lookup 95%; unstructured 65% vs FAIR fuzzy lookup 97%, loses -32pp). Strawman (+65pp vs 0% exact lookup) caught and corrected. Grounding delivered by lookup+constrained-decode+verifier, NOT the binder. MEASURED single-env; NOT PROVEN.

entries.requested: Integrity-audit clarification: distinguish pre-existing vs introduced bugs from the real-model campaign, and confirm the prior pushed campaign is unaffected.
entries.executed: Classified the two fixes from step_27. (1) CONTAMINATION FALSE-VETO: PRE-EXISTING in dcortex_professional/enforcement.py as committed+pushed on feature/professional-integration @7e45902 (line `len(nv)>=4 and nv in low`). Latent: the prior D_Cortex_PatentAnalyst campaign was UNAFFECTED (its verdict G3 committed_recall=1.0, G3 PASS, no false vetoes) because that pack had no value-substring overlap. It surfaced only on the IPC pack where single-word category values (e.g. AGRICULTURE) are substrings of titles. Fixed on feature/professional-real-model (now flags only a DIFFERENT entity's SAME-attribute value, len>=8). (2) CASE BUG (uppercase candidate codes vs normalized lowercase known_codes leaking A99 into the unanswerable set): INTRODUCED in this campaign's NEW file scripts/certify_professional_real.py (absent from the prior branch) and fixed within the same campaign before any commit.
entries.notes: Verification commands run: `git show feature/professional-integration:dcortex_professional/enforcement.py` (contamination check present => pre-existing); prior verdict committed_recall=1.0 G3=True (=> latent, not triggered, prior campaign verdict UNCHANGED); `git show feature/professional-integration:scripts/certify_professional_real.py` => fatal path not in branch (=> case bug introduced+fixed in this campaign). The enforcement.py improvement does not alter the prior pushed campaign's results; it only prevents the latent false-veto on packs with overlapping committed values. Memory was already updated in entry step_27; this entry adds the pre-existing-vs-introduced traceability the integrity audit asked for.

G_VETO_REAL captured a REAL fabricated Qwen IPC title ('General Methods or Articles Relating The') and force-abstained it. REGION B (VALID, fuzzy 46%<60%): fresh binder on Qwen layer-1 median 70% (5 seeds) vs exact 0% vs fuzzy 46% -> BEATS BOTH +24pp. This is the POSITIVE the corrected methodology (named capable model + model-known Region B) produces, inverse of the gpt2 negative. Validates [[model-choice-is-validity-critical]]. MEASURED single-env Qwen 4-bit; NOT PROVEN.

entries.requested: Validate the Region B binder win: held-out-by-fact split, shuffled-hidden-state ablation, MiniLM embedding baseline, >=10 seeds. Decide REAL vs artifact.
entries.executed: scripts/build_region_b_data.py (persist deterministic probe+clues), scripts/validate_region_b.py (4 baselines on held-out + 12-seed binder + ablation). Ran 5-agent adversarial audit (Workflow) before finalizing. Fixed make_clue token-overlap leakage and re-ran on cleaned data.
entries.notes: VERDICT REGION_B_BINDER_NEGATIVE (robust on cleaned data). Held-out (12 seeds, disjoint by fact): exact 0%, fuzzy 60%, MiniLM embedding 90%, BINDER median 63.3% [53%/72%]. Ablation 57% (collapses -> signal causal but WEAK, ~7pp above ablation). In-sample 100% (overfit, never reported). 5-agent audit (high confidence): binder NOT crippled, MiniLM FAIR, skews conservative. PRIOR d4e0b80 Region B 'win' RETRACTED -- it compared only vs crippled 0%/46% lookups, no embedding baseline. make_clue token-leak fixed (8 clues dropped); residual hallucinated-clue noise disclosed. Validates [[binder-claims-need-embedding-baseline]]. MEASURED single-env Qwen 4-bit; clean audited NEGATIVE.
